meta.name: DFI H&B Inspection Report Review – Rules Reference

checkpoints.requirement: Supplier Name / Factory Name — Cover page, Inspection Details — Both fields must be filled in English using the combined format: `Supplier Name (Factory Name)`. Neither field may contain Chinese characters or show only one of the two names.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify both fields independently
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify both fields independently

checkpoints.requirement: Product Name — Cover page / POs & product details, Name (Main identifier) — The product name field must show the real product name, not the SKU or barcode string. QIMAone auto-populates the SKU by default; the inspector must replace it manually. Check both the cover page and the POs & product details section.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify the same product name is consistent between cover and POs section
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify the same product name is consistent between cover and POs section

check_block.check: extract_bool("Is the product name a real product name (not a SKU/barcode string)?")

checkpoints.requirement: PO Number — Cover page, Inspection Details — If no PO was found on site, the field must read exactly "N/A" with no additional comment. The field must not be left blank.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify field value only
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify field value only

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field satisfy the GI requirement stated for this checkpoint?")

checkpoints.requirement: MFG Date / EXP Date / Batch Code — Cover page, Inspection Details — If all three date/batch fields are "N/A", they must be replaced with the sentence: "Refer to the individual batch/date code in the date code check section". If at least one field has a real value, no replacement is needed.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check with Date Code Check section to confirm codes are recorded there
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check with Date Code Check section to confirm codes are recorded there

check_block.check: extract_bool("If MFG/EXP/Batch are all N/A, is that combination explicitly justified in remarks?")

checkpoints.requirement: Location — Cover page, Inspection Details — The location field must be written entirely in English. No Chinese characters are permitted.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify language only
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify language only

checkpoints.requirement: Unit — Cover page / POs & product details — The unit field must not display "Other". If "Other" appears, it must be replaced with the actual unit found in the booking — typically "Boxes" or "Packs".
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify field value only
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify field value only

check_block.check: extract_bool("Is the quantity unit a real unit (Pcs/Boxes/Packs/…) and not 'Other'?")

checkpoints.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The overall result must match the Pass/Pending/Fail/Missing Inspection logic defined in Section 8. Verify that the result shown is consistent with all findings in the body of the report.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check against all test results, AQL outcomes, and measurement results
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check against all test results, AQL outcomes, and measurement results

checkpoints.requirement: Inspector's Remark — Cover page — Every Pending or Failed report must contain both of the following remarks: (1) "The inspection result is [Pending/Failed] due to [reason]." (2) "All defective items (and failure findings) are presented to the factory." Both must be present. Neither is optional.
checkpoints.scope_detail: result stated in remark must match the overall result on the cover
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — result stated in remark must match the overall result on the cover

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Inspector's Remark — Cover page — The remark must be in English. Chinese characters in inspector-written remark fields are not acceptable. Exception: date codes recorded exactly as printed on the product may appear in Chinese.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify language of remark text
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify language of remark text

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Attachments section — end of report — The Measurement Table Excel file must be attached. Verify its presence. Also verify that the filename does not contain Chinese characters — a Chinese filename prevents the report team from opening the file.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify presence and filename
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify presence and filename

checkpoints.requirement: Green Seal Sample (GSS) — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — The GSS result must be "Yes" (available on site and valid) for any report with an overall result of PASS. If GSS is absent, expired, or invalid, the report must be PENDING. Verify the GSS result is consistent with the overall result.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check GSS availability against overall result
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check GSS availability against overall result

checkpoints.requirement: Any remark, photo caption, or checklist field throughout the report — The term "GSS" is the only accepted terminology for the approval sample. "Approved Sample", "Golden Sample", or any equivalent must never appear anywhere in the report text or photo captions.
checkpoints.scope_detail: scan all text fields and photo captions
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — scan all text fields and photo captions

checkpoints.requirement: Re-Sealed Green Seal Sample — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — After using the GSS for comparison, the inspector must re-seal it in a polybag with a signed QIMA sticker showing the date and order number, and upload a photo. Verify the re-sealed GSS photo is present in the Product Specifications section.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check re-seal photo against GSS comparison photos (if comparison was done, re-seal must follow)
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check re-seal photo against GSS comparison photos (if comparison was done, re-seal must follow)

checkpoints.requirement: Carton Shipping Mark — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — Photos of the shipping mark must be present for both the front face and the side face of the carton. If an inner shipping mark exists, it must also be photographed.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify photo count and angles within Shipping Carton section
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify photo count and angles within Shipping Carton section

checkpoints.requirement: Carton Check: Dimensions and Weight — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — For each product style, the report must record: printed size (L×W×H in mm), actual measured size, printed weight (kg), and actual measured weight. If actual values differ from printed spec, a remark is required. A difference alone does not trigger Pending or Fail.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check recorded values against remark; verify remark is present when a difference exists
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check recorded values against remark; verify remark is present when a difference exists

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Individual Pack AQL table — Workmanship section — An Individual Pack AQL table must be present if and only if the product has individual packaging. If the product has no individual pack, the table must be absent. Verify against the product description and photos.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check table presence against product structure visible in photos and POs section
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check table presence against product structure visible in photos and POs section

checkpoints.requirement: Workmanship AQL tables — Workmanship section (Consumer Pack, Individual Pack, Destructive Sample) — Three separate AQL tables must be present: Consumer Pack, Individual Pack (if applicable), and Destructive Sample. Each must have its own result. Verify sampling levels match the standard: Consumer Pack = Level II (onsite) or Level I (remote); Individual Pack = S4 (restorable pack) or S3 (destructive/sticker); Destructive Sample = S1.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify each table independently
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify each table independently

checkpoints.requirement: Sample size and Max (acceptance number) — Workmanship AQL tables — The "Checked" number must match the sample size in the DFI Sampling Plan. The "Max" acceptance numbers must match: sample size 8 → 0/0/0; sample size 13 → 0/0/1; sample size 32 → 0/1/3. Destructive sample size is 8 if lot ≤ 35,000 and 13 if lot > 35,000.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check Checked, Max, and lot size within the same section
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check Checked, Max, and lot size within the same section

checkpoints.requirement: AQL values (CRI/MAJ/MIN) — Workmanship AQL tables — AQL values for general products must be Not Allowed / 1.5 / 4.0. Earplug products: same (Not Allowed / 1.5 / 4.0). Protection Mask products: percentage-based values must match the DFI Sampling Plan attachment exactly.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check AQL values against product type
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check AQL values against product type

checkpoints.requirement: Colors, Labels, Artworks, Accessories and Markings result — Workmanship section — If no special finding was observed in this category, the result must be "OK". QIMAone sometimes auto-generates "N/A" — this must be manually corrected to "OK" if there is no finding.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify result value
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify result value

checkpoints.requirement: Defect list and comments — Workmanship section — If no defects were found, the text "No Defects Found" must appear in the defect section. If defects were found, each defect must have at least one supporting photo with a QIMA sticker or arrow clearly indicating the defect.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check defect entries against photos
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check defect entries against photos

checkpoints.requirement: Defect comments and Important Remark — Workmanship section — If multiple defects were found on the same sample, only the most serious is counted in the AQL. A remark must appear in both the Defect Comment and the Important Remark following this template: "Found [X] pcs with multiple defects: [most serious] (counted into AQL) + [other] (excluded from AQL), see defect pictures below, all defects are arrowed."
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check defect count in AQL table against remark
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check defect count in AQL table against remark

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field satisfy the GI requirement stated for this checkpoint?")

checkpoints.requirement: AQL table and remark — Workmanship section — If a defect was found outside the workmanship check stage (e.g. during quantity check or dimension measurement), it must NOT appear in the AQL workmanship table. Instead, a remark must describe it separately, the report must be set to Pending, and the full consumer pack must be sealed in a polybag with a QIMA sticker.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check remark against AQL table entries and overall result
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check remark against AQL table entries and overall result

checkpoints.requirement: Date Code Check table — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — Date codes must be recorded exactly as printed: same format, separators, and casing. The format AND printing position must match between GSS and actual product. The actual values (dates, lot numbers) may differ — this is acceptable.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check GSS row against Actual Product row within the date code table
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check GSS row against Actual Product row within the date code table

checkpoints.requirement: Date Code Check table — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — At least one of the three codes must be present: MFG Date / EXP Date / Lot# (Batch#). If a code is absent on the product, record "N/A" in that field. If no date code AND no batch code is found on any packaging: this is a back-office escalation situation — verify that a remark and a Pending result are present.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check against overall result if all codes are N/A
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check against overall result if all codes are N/A

check_block.check: extract_bool("If MFG/EXP/Batch are all N/A, is that combination explicitly justified in remarks?")

checkpoints.requirement: Date Code Check table — multiple batches or sub-products — If the product has multiple batches or multiple sub-products (e.g. a travel set), all date codes must be clearly separated by batch and item keywords, with 1 batch per line. Chinese date codes must be recorded in Chinese exactly as printed.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify formatting of date code table entries
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify formatting of date code table entries

checkpoints.requirement: Barcode Details — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — The full barcode number must be recorded for each packaging level: Individual Pack / Consumer Pack / Outer Carton. If no Individual Pack exists, that field must show "N/A" — it must not be left blank.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify all three barcode fields are filled
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify all three barcode fields are filled

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement table — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Only specs explicitly claimed on the product name, label, or packaging must be measured. Do not measure specs not claimed anywhere. Both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack claimed specs must be measured. The Measurement Table is a template — only rows relevant to the actual product should be filled.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check measured specs against product label visible in photos
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check measured specs against product label visible in photos

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement results — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Apply pass/fail logic per spec type: (1) Volume, Quantity, Weight (general product): actual ≥ label claim → PASS; actual < label claim (even by 0.01) → FAIL. (2) Weight (health product — identified by NIP panel) and Size/Dimension: tolerance required; within tolerance → PASS; outside tolerance in either direction → FAIL.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check recorded values against pass/fail result and label claim
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check recorded values against pass/fail result and label claim

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement remark — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — For any tolerance-based check (dimension or health product weight), the tolerance range must be stated in the remark regardless of pass or fail outcome. Required format: "Checked OK, the tolerance range of [weight/dimension] is XXX – XXX."
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify remark is present whenever a tolerance-based measurement is recorded
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify remark is present whenever a tolerance-based measurement is recorded

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement table values — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — If all 8 (or 13) samples show exactly the same value for a measurement, flag as a likely data entry error. Real measurement variation across samples is expected — identical values across all samples are suspicious.
checkpoints.scope_detail: scan all measurement columns for uniform values
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — scan all measurement columns for uniform values

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement table — Protection Mask products — For Protection Mask products, the individual mask net weight must always be measured and recorded, even if no weight spec is claimed on the label.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify net weight row is present for Mask products
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify net weight row is present for Mask products

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field evidence satisfy: For Protection Mask products, the individual mask net weight must always be measured and recorded, even if no weight spe?")

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement table — Health products (products with NIP panel) — For health products (identified by a Nutrition Information Panel on the label), both total net weight and individual serving weight must be measured if the serving size per piece is stated on the NIP. Examples: "1 Capsule/1.043g" → weigh 1 capsule; "3 Tablets/5.4g" → weigh 3 tablets; "1370mg/softgel" → weigh 1 softgel.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check NIP label visible in photos against measurement rows
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check NIP label visible in photos against measurement rows

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field evidence satisfy: For health products (identified by a Nutrition Information Panel on the label), both total net weight and individual ser?")

checkpoints.requirement: Carton drop test result — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — The carton drop test result must follow this logic: PASS if no damage on product (carton dented but product OK = PASS); PENDING if carton broken/opened but product undamaged; FAIL if product is damaged or falls out. Any outcome other than a fully clean pass requires a descriptive remark.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check result against remark description
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check result against remark description

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Carton drop test result — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — The carton drop test is mandatory for all products. Exceptions: fragile products (ceramics, glass — factory documentation must be checked), and product "Guardian WEIGHING SCALE BR9012" from supplier "Camry Electronic". If the factory refused: result must be PENDING with a remark.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check against product type and overall result
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check against product type and overall result

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Adhesive test result — test checklist (e.g. DCL 013 - Dairy Farm - Toiletry V9 H&B) — An adhesive test failure (printing unreadable after tape removal) must result in overall report = PENDING, never FAIL. Verify consistency between adhesive test result and overall result.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check adhesive test result against overall result
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check adhesive test result against overall result

checkpoints.requirement: Adhesive test remark — test checklist — The tape model used must be stated in the remark. Accepted models: 3M 500, 3M 600P, 3M 810.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify remark contains tape model reference
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify remark contains tape model reference

check: extract("Quote the tape model stated in this comment, or null")

checkpoints.requirement: Adhesive test remark — test checklist (exemption products) — For products on the adhesive test date code exemption list (cotton tips in canisters from SOFT TIP (THAILAND) CO., LTD and WISE EVER ENTERPRISES LIMITED; PE bag products including Gurdian/Mannings amino acid bubble cleansing towel and Mannings Face Towel 10s), the date code area is exempt. If this exemption applies, a mandatory remark must be added: "Adhesive test on the date code waived due to limitation of the can and ink." All other printed areas on these products must still be tested.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify exemption remark is present when applicable
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify exemption remark is present when applicable

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?")

checkpoints.requirement: Rubbing test result — test checklist — The rubbing test must be performed on all printed areas and markings on both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present). The test must be performed with both dry cloth (10 strokes, 9N force) and wet cloth. Result = PASS if printing clear and cloth unstained; FAIL if printing rubbed off, dimmed, or illegible.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check result against photos showing cloth and product surface
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check result against photos showing cloth and product surface

checkpoints.requirement: Cover photo — Cover page — The cover photo must show the Consumer Pack (retail packaging), frontal angle, vertical orientation, with the full product name clearly readable.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify photo content
checkpoints.context: QUESTION — verify photo content

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field evidence satisfy: The cover photo must show the Consumer Pack (retail packaging), frontal angle, vertical orientation, with the full produ?")

checkpoints.requirement: GSS photos — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — A photo of the GSS showing the DFI QA Team label with signature must be present. The re-sealed GSS photo (polybag + signed QIMA sticker) must also be present.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify both GSS-related photos within the Product Specifications section
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify both GSS-related photos within the Product Specifications section

checkpoints.requirement: GSS comparison photos — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — GSS vs actual product comparison photos must cover: all four faces (top, bottom, front, back) for Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present); date code area close-up; composition/ingredient label; volume or quantity claims; all barcodes and markings; product inside (capsules, liquid, etc.). Each element must appear in a photo.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify completeness of comparison photo set
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify completeness of comparison photo set

checkpoints.requirement: Measurement photos — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Every spec that was measured must have at least one photo per sample (minimum 8 photos for a sample size of 8). Weight photos must show the scale display readable with the product on the scale. Dimension photos must show the measuring tool and product.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check number of photos against sample size and number of measured specs
checkpoints.context: SECTION — cross-check number of photos against sample size and number of measured specs

checkpoints.requirement: Carton drop test photos — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — Three photo stages are required: (1) before drop showing intact carton; (2) during drop; (3) after drop showing carton exterior condition AND interior product condition. Interior product photo is especially critical when any damage or deformation is observed.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify all three photo stages are present
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify all three photo stages are present

checkpoints.requirement: Adhesive and Rubbing test photos — test checklist — Adhesive test photos must clearly show the tape being applied or removed on the date code area specifically, for both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present). Rubbing test photos must show the cloth on the product surface.
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify photo content and coverage
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify photo content and coverage

checkpoints.requirement: Factory Review photos — QIMA Factory Review checklist — The following photos must be present: factory entrance/gate (confirming location), inspection room lighting, weighing scale and measuring tools with calibration certificates visible, and all three signed QIMA documents (Factory Disclaimer, Draft Report, COC).
checkpoints.scope_detail: verify all four photo categories within Factory Review
checkpoints.context: SECTION — verify all four photo categories within Factory Review

checkpoints.requirement: Any photo throughout the report — No photo in the report may show a worker's face, name badge, or ID in a way that identifies the individual. If visible, this is a data privacy issue.
checkpoints.scope_detail: scan all photos
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — scan all photos

check_block.check: extract_bool("Does this field evidence satisfy: No photo in the report may show a worker's face, name badge, or ID in a way that identifies the individual. If visible, ?")

checkpoints.requirement: AQL tables — Workmanship section — Verify the AQL levels and acceptance numbers match the product type and inspection mode (onsite vs remote):
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check product type, inspection mode, and AQL values
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check product type, inspection mode, and AQL values

checkpoints.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The overall result must follow this logic exactly.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check overall result against all findings across all sections
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check overall result against all findings across all sections

checkpoints.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The adhesive test is the only test where failure triggers PENDING rather than FAIL. All other test failures (rubbing test, function test, sensory check, barcode scanning, back adhesive test for pantyliners, bristle pull test) trigger FAIL. Verify the overall result matches this distinction.
checkpoints.scope_detail: cross-check test results against overall result
checkpoints.context: FULL REPORT — cross-check test results against overall result
